title: Compose Basics
description: Jetpack Compose fundamentals and state management
lesson:
Declarative UI with Compose

Jetpack Compose replaces XML layouts with Kotlin functions that describe UI. Instead of imperatively mutating views (textView.text = "Hello"), you declare what the UI should look like for a given state, and Compose handles the updates automatically.

Mental Model Shift: In the View system, you create a widget and mutate it over time. In Compose, you describe the UI for a given state. When state changes, the framework recomposes — re-calls your functions to produce a new UI description. You never call setText() or setVisibility().

Core Composables — The Building Blocks

Compose provides a set of fundamental composables. Every screen is built from combinations of these:

ComposableView EquivalentPurpose
ColumnLinearLayout (vertical)Stack children vertically
RowLinearLayout (horizontal)Stack children horizontally
BoxFrameLayoutStack children on top of each other (z-axis)
Surface—Material container with shape, color, elevation
ScaffoldCoordinatorLayoutMaterial screen structure: topBar, bottomBar, FAB, snackbar, content
TextTextViewDisplay text with styling
ImageImageViewDisplay images (painter, contentDescription)
ButtonButtonClickable button with Material styling
TextFieldEditTextText input (controlled component — requires state)
CardCardViewElevated container with rounded corners
LazyColumnRecyclerViewScrollable list — only composes visible items
LazyRowRecyclerView (horizontal)Horizontal scrollable list

Layout Composables — Deep Dive

Understanding the layout composables and their arrangement/alignment options is essential for building any screen.

Column — Vertical Stacking

Column places children top-to-bottom. Control spacing with verticalArrangement and horizontal positioning with horizontalAlignment.

// verticalArrangement controls how children are spaced vertically Column( modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(), verticalArrangement = Arrangement.SpacedBy(8.dp), // fixed gap between items horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally ) { Text("Item 1") Text("Item 2") Text("Item 3") } // All verticalArrangement options: Arrangement.Top // pack to top (default) Arrangement.Center // center vertically Arrangement.Bottom // pack to bottom Arrangement.SpaceEvenly // equal space before, between, and after Arrangement.SpaceBetween // equal space between items, none at edges Arrangement.SpaceAround // equal space around each item Arrangement.SpacedBy(8.dp) // fixed spacing between each item // All horizontalAlignment options: Alignment.Start // align left (LTR) Alignment.CenterHorizontally // center horizontally Alignment.End // align right (LTR)
Column verticalArrangement comparison (fillMaxHeight): Top Center Bottom SpaceEvenly SpaceBetween ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ │ [Item1] │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Item1] │ │ [Item2] │ │ │ │ │ │ [Item1] │ │ │ │ [Item3] │ │ [Item1] │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Item2] │ │ │ │ [Item2] │ │ [Item2] │ │ │ │ [Item3] │ │ [Item1] │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Item2] │ │ [Item3] │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Item3] │ │ │ │ [Item3] │ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘

Row — Horizontal Stacking

Row places children left-to-right. Control spacing with horizontalArrangement and vertical positioning with verticalAlignment.

Row( modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(), horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween, verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically ) { Icon(Icons.Default.Menu, contentDescription = "Menu") Text("Title", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleLarge) Icon(Icons.Default.Search, contentDescription = "Search") } // horizontalArrangement options (mirrors Column's vertical): Arrangement.Start // pack to start (default) Arrangement.Center // center horizontally Arrangement.End // pack to end Arrangement.SpaceEvenly // equal space before, between, and after Arrangement.SpaceBetween // equal space between items, none at edges Arrangement.SpaceAround // equal space around each item Arrangement.SpacedBy(12.dp)// fixed spacing between each item // verticalAlignment options: Alignment.Top // align to top Alignment.CenterVertically // center vertically Alignment.Bottom // align to bottom

Box — Layered Stacking (Z-Axis)

Box stacks children on top of each other. The last child is drawn on top. Use contentAlignment to position all children, or Modifier.align() on individual children.

Box( modifier = Modifier .size(200.dp) .background(Color.LightGray), contentAlignment = Alignment.Center // default position for ALL children ) { Image( painter = painterResource(R.drawable.photo), contentDescription = null, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(), contentScale = ContentScale.Crop ) // Badge in top-end corner — overrides contentAlignment Badge( modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.TopEnd) ) // Label at bottom Text( "Overlay text", modifier = Modifier .align(Alignment.BottomCenter) .background(Color.Black.copy(alpha = 0.6f)) .padding(4.dp), color = Color.White ) }
Box contentAlignment — all 9 positions: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ TopStart TopCenter TopEnd │ │ │ │ │ │ CenterStart Center CenterEnd │ │ │ │ │ │ BottomStart BottomCenter BottomEnd │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ Box(contentAlignment = Alignment.TopEnd) places ALL children at the top-right by default. Individual children can override with Modifier.align().
propagateMinConstraints: When set to true, Box forces its minimum constraints onto all children. This is useful when you want every child to fill the box (e.g., a background image must be at least as large as the Box). Surface uses this internally.

Spacer — Empty Space

Spacer is an empty composable used to add fixed or flexible space between items.

// Fixed-size spacer — always 16dp tall Column { Text("Above") Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(16.dp)) Text("Below") } // Weight-based spacer — pushes content apart Column(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxHeight()) { Text("Top content") Spacer(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) // takes all remaining space Button(onClick = {}) { Text("Bottom button") } } // Row with spacer pushing icon to the end Row(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) { Text("Title") Spacer(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) Icon(Icons.Default.ChevronRight, null) }

Surface — Material Container

Surface is the Material Design container. It clips content to its shape, applies color and elevation, and handles content color.

Surface( modifier = Modifier.padding(8.dp), shape = RoundedCornerShape(16.dp), // clips content to shape color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface, // background color tonalElevation = 2.dp, // tonal color shift (M3) shadowElevation = 4.dp // drop shadow ) { Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) { Text("Card Title", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium) Text("Card content goes here") } } // Clickable surface (acts like a card button) Surface( onClick = { navigateToDetail() }, shape = RoundedCornerShape(12.dp), tonalElevation = 1.dp ) { Row(modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) { Text("Tap me", modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) Icon(Icons.Default.ChevronRight, null) } }
tonalElevation vs shadowElevation: In Material 3, tonalElevation changes the surface color slightly (higher = more tinted with primary). shadowElevation adds a traditional drop shadow. You can use both independently.

Scaffold — Full Screen Structure

Scaffold provides the standard Material screen layout with slots for top bar, bottom bar, FAB, snackbar, and content.

@Composable fun MainScreen() { val snackbarHostState = remember { SnackbarHostState() } val scope = rememberCoroutineScope() Scaffold( topBar = { TopAppBar( title = { Text("My App") }, navigationIcon = { IconButton(onClick = { /* open drawer */ }) { Icon(Icons.Default.Menu, "Menu") } }, actions = { IconButton(onClick = { /* search */ }) { Icon(Icons.Default.Search, "Search") } IconButton(onClick = { /* more */ }) { Icon(Icons.Default.MoreVert, "More") } } ) }, bottomBar = { NavigationBar { NavigationBarItem( selected = true, onClick = { }, icon = { Icon(Icons.Default.Home, "Home") }, label = { Text("Home") } ) NavigationBarItem( selected = false, onClick = { }, icon = { Icon(Icons.Default.Settings, "Settings") }, label = { Text("Settings") } ) } }, floatingActionButton = { FloatingActionButton( onClick = { scope.launch { snackbarHostState.showSnackbar("Item added!") } } ) { Icon(Icons.Default.Add, "Add") } }, snackbarHost = { SnackbarHost(snackbarHostState) }, contentWindowInsets = WindowInsets.safeDrawing // handle system bars ) { innerPadding -> // IMPORTANT: always apply innerPadding to content! LazyColumn( modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding) ) { items(50) { index -> Text( "Item #$index", modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .padding(16.dp) ) } } } }
Always use innerPadding! Scaffold passes innerPadding (PaddingValues) to the content lambda. This accounts for the top bar, bottom bar, and system insets. Ignoring it causes content to render behind the bars.

Text Composables — Deep Dive

Text — Display and Styling

The Text composable is the workhorse for displaying styled text.

// Basic text with styling Text( text = "Hello, Compose!", style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineMedium, // M3 type scale color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary, fontSize = 20.sp, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold, fontFamily = FontFamily.Serif, letterSpacing = 0.5.sp, lineHeight = 28.sp, maxLines = 2, overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis, // Ellipsis, Clip, or Visible textAlign = TextAlign.Center, textDecoration = TextDecoration.Underline // or LineThrough, combine with + ) // AnnotatedString — mixed styles and clickable spans val annotated = buildAnnotatedString { append("By signing up, you agree to our ") pushStringAnnotation(tag = "URL", annotation = "https://example.com/terms") withStyle(SpanStyle(color = Color.Blue, textDecoration = TextDecoration.Underline)) { append("Terms of Service") } pop() append(" and ") pushStringAnnotation(tag = "URL", annotation = "https://example.com/privacy") withStyle(SpanStyle(color = Color.Blue, textDecoration = TextDecoration.Underline)) { append("Privacy Policy") } pop() } ClickableText( text = annotated, onClick = { offset -> annotated.getStringAnnotations("URL", offset, offset) .firstOrNull()?.let { annotation -> uriHandler.openUri(annotation.item) } } )

TextField and OutlinedTextField — Text Input

Compose text fields are controlled components — you must provide the current value and handle changes.

// OutlinedTextField — most common in Material 3 var email by remember { mutableStateOf("") } var password by remember { mutableStateOf("") } var isError by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } OutlinedTextField( value = email, onValueChange = { email = it isError = !it.contains("@") }, label = { Text("Email") }, placeholder = { Text("user@example.com") }, leadingIcon = { Icon(Icons.Default.Email, null) }, trailingIcon = { if (isError) Icon(Icons.Default.Warning, "Error", tint = Color.Red) }, isError = isError, supportingText = { if (isError) Text("Invalid email address", color = Color.Red) }, keyboardOptions = KeyboardOptions( keyboardType = KeyboardType.Email, imeAction = ImeAction.Next ), keyboardActions = KeyboardActions( onNext = { focusManager.moveFocus(FocusDirection.Down) } ), singleLine = true, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth() ) // Password field with visibility toggle var passwordVisible by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } OutlinedTextField( value = password, onValueChange = { password = it }, label = { Text("Password") }, visualTransformation = if (passwordVisible) VisualTransformation.None else PasswordVisualTransformation(), trailingIcon = { IconButton(onClick = { passwordVisible = !passwordVisible }) { Icon( if (passwordVisible) Icons.Default.VisibilityOff else Icons.Default.Visibility, "Toggle password visibility" ) } }, keyboardOptions = KeyboardOptions( keyboardType = KeyboardType.Password, imeAction = ImeAction.Done ), singleLine = true, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth() )
TextField vs OutlinedTextField vs BasicTextField:
- TextField — filled style (background, no border)
- OutlinedTextField — outlined style (border, no fill) — most common in M3
- BasicTextField — no Material styling; use for fully custom input fields. You provide all decorations.

Interactive Composables

Buttons — All Variants

// Filled button — primary action Button(onClick = { submit() }) { Icon(Icons.Default.Send, null, modifier = Modifier.size(18.dp)) Spacer(Modifier.width(8.dp)) Text("Submit") } // Outlined button — secondary action OutlinedButton(onClick = { cancel() }) { Text("Cancel") } // Text button — tertiary/low-emphasis action TextButton(onClick = { skipOnboarding() }) { Text("Skip") } // Icon button — toolbar or compact actions IconButton(onClick = { toggleFavorite() }) { Icon( imageVector = if (isFav) Icons.Filled.Favorite else Icons.Outlined.FavoriteBorder, contentDescription = "Favorite", tint = if (isFav) Color.Red else Color.Gray ) } // FAB — primary floating action FloatingActionButton(onClick = { addItem() }) { Icon(Icons.Default.Add, "Add") } // Extended FAB — FAB with text label ExtendedFloatingActionButton( onClick = { compose() }, icon = { Icon(Icons.Default.Edit, null) }, text = { Text("Compose") } )

Selection Controls

// Checkbox var checked by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } Row(verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically) { Checkbox(checked = checked, onCheckedChange = { checked = it }) Text("Accept terms", modifier = Modifier.clickable { checked = !checked }) } // Switch var enabled by remember { mutableStateOf(true) } Row( modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(), horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween, verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically ) { Text("Notifications") Switch(checked = enabled, onCheckedChange = { enabled = it }) } // RadioButton group pattern val options = listOf("Light", "Dark", "System") var selectedOption by remember { mutableStateOf(options[0]) } Column { options.forEach { option -> Row( modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .clickable { selectedOption = option } .padding(vertical = 8.dp), verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically ) { RadioButton( selected = (option == selectedOption), onClick = { selectedOption = option } ) Text(option, modifier = Modifier.padding(start = 8.dp)) } } } // Slider var volume by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0.5f) } Slider( value = volume, onValueChange = { volume = it }, valueRange = 0f..1f, steps = 10 // snap to discrete positions (0 = continuous) ) // RangeSlider var priceRange by remember { mutableStateOf(10f..100f) } RangeSlider( value = priceRange, onValueChange = { priceRange = it }, valueRange = 0f..200f )

DropdownMenu and AlertDialog

// DropdownMenu var expanded by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } var selectedText by remember { mutableStateOf("Select option") } Box { OutlinedButton(onClick = { expanded = true }) { Text(selectedText) Icon(Icons.Default.ArrowDropDown, null) } DropdownMenu(expanded = expanded, onDismissRequest = { expanded = false }) { listOf("Option A", "Option B", "Option C").forEach { option -> DropdownMenuItem( text = { Text(option) }, onClick = { selectedText = option expanded = false } ) } } } // AlertDialog var showDialog by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } if (showDialog) { AlertDialog( onDismissRequest = { showDialog = false }, title = { Text("Delete Item?") }, text = { Text("This action cannot be undone.") }, confirmButton = { TextButton(onClick = { deleteItem() showDialog = false }) { Text("Delete", color = Color.Red) } }, dismissButton = { TextButton(onClick = { showDialog = false }) { Text("Cancel") } } ) } // ModalBottomSheet var showSheet by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } val sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState() if (showSheet) { ModalBottomSheet( onDismissRequest = { showSheet = false }, sheetState = sheetState ) { Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) { Text("Sort by", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium) Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp)) listOf("Name", "Date", "Size").forEach { option -> Row( modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .clickable { showSheet = false } .padding(vertical = 12.dp) ) { Text(option) } } Spacer(Modifier.height(32.dp)) // bottom padding for nav bar } } }

Image and Media Composables

// Image from resources Image( painter = painterResource(id = R.drawable.photo), contentDescription = "Profile photo", modifier = Modifier .size(120.dp) .clip(CircleShape) .border(2.dp, Color.Gray, CircleShape), contentScale = ContentScale.Crop ) // AsyncImage with Coil (network images) AsyncImage( model = ImageRequest.Builder(LocalContext.current) .data("https://example.com/photo.jpg") .crossfade(true) .build(), contentDescription = "User photo", placeholder = painterResource(R.drawable.placeholder), error = painterResource(R.drawable.error_image), contentScale = ContentScale.Crop, modifier = Modifier .size(80.dp) .clip(RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)) ) // Icon — Material icons or custom vectors Icon( imageVector = Icons.Filled.Favorite, contentDescription = "Favorite", tint = Color.Red, modifier = Modifier.size(24.dp) )
ContentScale options: Original Image: wider than container ┌───────────────────┐ │ Image Data │ └───────────────────┘ Placed in: ┌────────┐ │Container│ └────────┘ Crop Fit FillBounds Inside None ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ ┌────────┐ │████████│ │ │ │████████│ │ │ │ │ │████████│ │ ██████ │ │████████│ │ ██████ │ │███████████ │████████│ │ ██████ │ │████████│ │ ██████ │ │███████████ │████████│ │ │ │████████│ │ │ │ │ └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ fills & fits fully stretches fits fully no scaling, clips (aspect OK) (distorts!) (may be may overflow smaller)

The Modifier System — Deep Dive

Modifiers configure size, layout, behavior, appearance, and interaction for any composable. They chain together — and ORDER MATTERS.

Modifier order changes the result! Each modifier wraps the one after it, from outside in. padding before background adds space outside the background (border effect). padding after background adds space inside the background (inner padding).
Modifier.padding(16.dp).background(Red) Modifier.background(Red).padding(16.dp) ┌──────────────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ 16dp empty padding │ │ ████████████████████████ │ │ ┌────────────────────┐ │ │ ██ 16dp RED padding ██ │ │ │ ██ RED background █│ │ │ ██ ┌────────────────┐██ │ │ │ ████████████████████│ │ │ ██ │ content │██ │ │ └────────────────────┘ │ │ ██ └────────────────┘██ │ └──────────────────────────┘ │ ████████████████████████ │ └──────────────────────────┘ padding is OUTSIDE color padding is INSIDE color

Size Modifiers

// Fill parent Modifier.fillMaxWidth() // width = match_parent Modifier.fillMaxHeight() // height = match_parent Modifier.fillMaxSize() // both width and height Modifier.fillMaxWidth(0.5f) // 50% of parent width // Fixed size Modifier.width(200.dp) // exact width Modifier.height(48.dp) // exact height Modifier.size(48.dp) // both width and height Modifier.size(width = 200.dp, height = 48.dp) // different w/h // Required size — ignores parent constraints Modifier.requiredWidth(300.dp) // ALWAYS 300dp, even if parent is smaller Modifier.requiredHeight(100.dp) Modifier.requiredSize(50.dp) // Wrap content Modifier.wrapContentWidth() // shrink to content width Modifier.wrapContentHeight() // shrink to content height Modifier.wrapContentSize() // both // Min size constraint Modifier.defaultMinSize(minWidth = 48.dp, minHeight = 48.dp) // touch target // Weight in Row/Column — distributes remaining space Row(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) { Text("Fixed", modifier = Modifier.width(80.dp)) Text("Flex 2", modifier = Modifier.weight(2f)) // 2/3 of remaining Text("Flex 1", modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) // 1/3 of remaining }
Weight distribution in Row (total width = 300dp): Text("Fixed") Text(weight=2f) Text(weight=1f) width=80dp gets 2/3 remaining gets 1/3 remaining ├── 80dp ──├──── ~146dp ────├──── ~74dp ────┤ ┌──────────┬────────────────┬───────────────┐ │ Fixed │ Flex 2 │ Flex 1 │ └──────────┴────────────────┴───────────────┘ remaining = 300 - 80 = 220dp weight 2f = 220 * 2/3 = ~146dp weight 1f = 220 * 1/3 = ~74dp

Padding and Offset

// Padding — adds space INSIDE the element's bounds Modifier.padding(16.dp) // all sides Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp) Modifier.padding(start = 16.dp, top = 8.dp, end = 16.dp, bottom = 8.dp) Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp) // left + right only Modifier.padding(vertical = 8.dp) // top + bottom only // absolutePadding — ignores RTL layout direction Modifier.absolutePadding(left = 16.dp, right = 8.dp) // Offset — shifts position WITHOUT affecting layout // Other elements still see the ORIGINAL position Modifier.offset(x = 10.dp, y = (-5).dp) // RTL-aware (x flips) Modifier.absoluteOffset(x = 10.dp, y = 0.dp) // ignores RTL
Padding vs Offset: padding changes the measured size — siblings react to it. offset shifts the element visually without changing its measured size. Other elements still lay out as if the offset element were in its original position. Use offset for overlapping effects or badge positioning.

Alignment Modifiers

// Modifier.align — only works in specific scopes // In Box: position this child within the Box Box(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) { Text("TopStart", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.TopStart)) Text("Center", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.Center)) Text("BottomEnd", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.BottomEnd)) } // In Row: align this child vertically Row(modifier = Modifier.height(100.dp)) { Text("Top", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.Top)) Text("Center", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.CenterVertically)) Text("Bottom", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.Bottom)) } // In Row: align by text baseline Row { Text("Big", fontSize = 30.sp, modifier = Modifier.alignByBaseline()) Text("small", fontSize = 12.sp, modifier = Modifier.alignByBaseline()) // Both texts sit on the same baseline, not top-aligned } // In Column: align this child horizontally Column(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) { Text("Start", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.Start)) Text("Center", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.CenterHorizontally)) Text("End", modifier = Modifier.align(Alignment.End)) }

Drawing Modifiers

// Background Modifier.background(Color.Blue) // solid color Modifier.background(Color.Blue, RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)) // with shape Modifier.background(brush = Brush.horizontalGradient( // gradient colors = listOf(Color.Blue, Color.Cyan) )) // Border Modifier.border(1.dp, Color.Gray) // rectangular Modifier.border(2.dp, Color.Blue, RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)) // rounded Modifier.border(2.dp, Brush.sweepGradient( // gradient border listOf(Color.Red, Color.Blue, Color.Green) ), CircleShape) // Clip — clips content to a shape Modifier.clip(RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)) // rounded rect Modifier.clip(CircleShape) // circle Modifier.clip(CutCornerShape(8.dp)) // cut corners (diamond-like) Modifier.clip(RoundedCornerShape( // asymmetric topStart = 16.dp, topEnd = 16.dp, bottomStart = 0.dp, bottomEnd = 0.dp )) // Shadow — must come BEFORE clip for proper rendering Modifier .shadow(8.dp, RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)) // shadow then clip .clip(RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)) .background(Color.White) // Visual transformations Modifier.alpha(0.5f) // 50% opacity Modifier.rotate(45f) // rotate 45 degrees Modifier.scale(1.5f) // scale 150% Modifier.scale(scaleX = 1f, scaleY = 0.5f) // non-uniform

Interaction Modifiers

// clickable — tap handler with ripple Modifier.clickable { handleClick() } Modifier.clickable( enabled = isEnabled, onClickLabel = "Open settings", // accessibility role = Role.Button, onClick = { handleClick() } ) // combinedClickable — tap, long press, and double tap Modifier.combinedClickable( onClick = { selectItem() }, onLongClick = { showContextMenu() }, onDoubleClick = { quickAction() } ) // selectable — for single-selection groups Modifier.selectable( selected = isSelected, onClick = { onSelect() }, role = Role.RadioButton ) // toggleable — for on/off controls Modifier.toggleable( value = isToggled, onValueChange = { isToggled = it }, role = Role.Checkbox ) // draggable — one-axis drag var offsetX by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) } Modifier.draggable( state = rememberDraggableState { delta -> offsetX += delta }, orientation = Orientation.Horizontal )

Scroll Modifiers

// Make any Column/Row scrollable (for non-lazy content) val scrollState = rememberScrollState() Column( modifier = Modifier .fillMaxSize() .verticalScroll(scrollState) // entire column scrolls ) { repeat(50) { index -> Text("Item #$index", modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) } } // Horizontal scroll Row( modifier = Modifier.horizontalScroll(rememberScrollState()) ) { repeat(20) { index -> Card(modifier = Modifier.size(100.dp).padding(8.dp)) { Text("Card $index") } } }
verticalScroll vs LazyColumn: Use verticalScroll for small, fixed-size lists (all items composed immediately). Use LazyColumn for large/dynamic lists (only visible items composed). Nesting a LazyColumn inside a verticalScroll Column will crash — they both want to control scrolling.

Focus Modifiers

val focusRequester = remember { FocusRequester() } var isFocused by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } OutlinedTextField( value = text, onValueChange = { text = it }, modifier = Modifier .focusRequester(focusRequester) // attach requester .onFocusChanged { focusState -> isFocused = focusState.isFocused // react to focus changes } .border( width = if (isFocused) 2.dp else 1.dp, color = if (isFocused) Color.Blue else Color.Gray ) ) // Programmatically request focus (e.g., on screen load) LaunchedEffect(Unit) { focusRequester.requestFocus() }

Modifier Cheat Sheet

ModifierWhat It DoesExample
.fillMaxWidth()Width matches parentModifier.fillMaxWidth()
.fillMaxSize()Both dimensions match parentModifier.fillMaxSize()
.size()Fixed width and heightModifier.size(48.dp)
.weight()Flex space in Row/ColumnModifier.weight(1f)
.padding()Inner spacing (affects layout)Modifier.padding(16.dp)
.offset()Visual shift (no layout change)Modifier.offset(x = 4.dp)
.background()Fill background color/gradientModifier.background(Color.Blue)
.border()Draw border outlineModifier.border(1.dp, Color.Gray)
.clip()Clip to shapeModifier.clip(CircleShape)
.shadow()Drop shadowModifier.shadow(4.dp)
.alpha()Opacity (0f-1f)Modifier.alpha(0.5f)
.rotate()Rotation in degreesModifier.rotate(45f)
.scale()Scale factorModifier.scale(1.5f)
.clickable {}Tap with rippleModifier.clickable { doIt() }
.combinedClickable()Tap + long press + double tapModifier.combinedClickable(onClick={}, onLongClick={})
.verticalScroll()Make content scrollable verticallyModifier.verticalScroll(rememberScrollState())
.horizontalScroll()Make content scrollable horizontallyModifier.horizontalScroll(rememberScrollState())
.focusRequester()Attach focus requesterModifier.focusRequester(requester)
.onFocusChanged {}React to focus eventsModifier.onFocusChanged { state -> }
.align()Position within Box/Row/ColumnModifier.align(Alignment.Center)
.alignByBaseline()Align text baselines in RowModifier.alignByBaseline()
.defaultMinSize()Minimum touch targetModifier.defaultMinSize(48.dp)
.wrapContentSize()Shrink to contentModifier.wrapContentSize()
.requiredSize()Ignores parent constraintsModifier.requiredSize(300.dp)
.draggable()One-axis drag handlingModifier.draggable(state, Horizontal)

State and Recomposition

When state changes, Compose recomposes — it re-calls your composable functions to produce an updated UI. Two things are required for state to work:

State in Compose requires TWO things: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 1. REMEMBER → preserves value across recompositions (without it, variable resets every time) 2. MUTABLE STATE OF → makes Compose OBSERVE the value (without it, changes don't trigger recomposition) var count by remember { mutableStateOf(0) } ──────── ─────────────── keeps it makes it alive observable
@Composable fun Greeting(name: String) { // remember: survives recomposition // mutableStateOf: triggers recomposition on change var count by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) } Column { Text("Hello $name! Clicked $count times") Button(onClick = { count++ }) { Text("Click me") } } } // BAD: resets to 0 on every recomposition var count = 0 // BAD: remembered but NOT observable — changes don't update UI var count by remember { 0 } // GOOD: remembered AND observable var count by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }

State Hoisting — The Pattern You Will Use Everywhere

State hoisting means moving state up to a caller and passing events down. This makes composables reusable, testable, and previewable:

State Hoisting (Unidirectional Data Flow) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ┌──────────────────────┐ │ Parent (Stateful) │ │ var text = "..." │ │ │ │ state ──────┐ │ │ ▼ │ │ ┌────────────────┐ │ │ │ Child │ │ │ │ (Stateless) │ │ │ │ text: String │ │ │ │ onTextChange() │──── events flow UP │ └────────────────┘ │ └──────────────────────┘ state flows DOWN
// Stateless composable — reusable, previewable, testable @Composable fun SearchBar( query: String, // state flows DOWN onQueryChange: (String) -> Unit // events flow UP ) { TextField( value = query, onValueChange = onQueryChange, label = { Text("Search") } ) } // Stateful parent — owns and manages the state @Composable fun SearchScreen() { var query by remember { mutableStateOf("") } SearchBar(query = query, onQueryChange = { query = it }) }

remember vs rememberSaveable

rememberrememberSaveable
Survives recompositionYesYes
Survives config change (rotation)No — state lost!Yes — saved to Bundle
Survives process deathNoYes (if Saver provided)
Works withAny typePrimitives, Parcelable, or custom Saver
Use forDerived values, animation stateUser input, scroll position, toggle state
Rule of thumb: If the user would be frustrated to lose the state on rotation, use rememberSaveable. For example: text they typed, a tab they selected, a scroll position. For computed values or animation state, remember is fine.
// User types "Hello" then rotates the phone... var text by remember { mutableStateOf("") } // LOST — resets to "" var text by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") } // KEPT — still "Hello" // For complex objects, provide a custom Saver: val user by rememberSaveable(stateSaver = autoSaver()) { mutableStateOf(User("Alice", 30)) }

Recomposition — What Triggers It and How to Control It

Recomposition is the process of re-calling composable functions when their inputs change. Understanding it is critical for performance.

What triggers recomposition? ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 1. A State<T> value that is READ by the composable changes 2. A parameter passed to the composable changes What does NOT trigger recomposition? ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ - Changing a regular variable (no observation) - State that is NOT read in the composable - Parameters that are the same (equals) — SKIPPED

Compose can skip recomposition of a composable if all its parameters are unchanged. For this to work, parameters must be stable:

// STABLE — Compose can skip recomposition if unchanged data class User(val name: String, val age: Int) // primitives + String = stable // UNSTABLE — Compose CANNOT skip (List is mutable interface) data class Feed(val posts: List<Post>) // List is NOT stable // Fix: Use @Immutable or @Stable annotations @Immutable data class Feed(val posts: List<Post>) // Promise: once created, never changes @Stable class FormState { // Promise: changes are observed by Compose var name by mutableStateOf("") var email by mutableStateOf("") }
@Immutable vs @Stable: @Immutable = the object never changes after creation. @Stable = the object may change, but changes are always observable via Compose state. Both let Compose skip recomposition. Use @Immutable for data classes, @Stable for classes with mutableStateOf properties.

Composable Function Rules

Composable functions have specific constraints you must follow:

Rules for @Composable functions:
1. No side effects in the body — don't write to DB, make network calls, or modify global state directly. Use side effect APIs (LaunchedEffect, etc.).
2. Idempotent — calling the function multiple times with the same inputs must produce the same UI.
3. Can execute in any order — siblings are not guaranteed to run top-to-bottom.
4. Can run in parallel — Compose may recompose multiple composables on different threads.
5. Can be skipped — if inputs haven't changed, the function might not be called at all.
6. Can run frequently — on every frame during animations. Keep them fast.

Complete Screen Examples

Login Screen — TextField, Button, Error States, Loading

@Composable fun LoginScreen( onLoginSuccess: () -> Unit ) { var email by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") } var password by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") } var passwordVisible by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } var isLoading by remember { mutableStateOf(false) } var errorMessage by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) } val focusManager = LocalFocusManager.current Column( modifier = Modifier .fillMaxSize() .padding(24.dp), verticalArrangement = Arrangement.Center, horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally ) { Text( "Welcome Back", style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineLarge, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold ) Spacer(Modifier.height(32.dp)) // Email field OutlinedTextField( value = email, onValueChange = { email = it; errorMessage = null }, label = { Text("Email") }, leadingIcon = { Icon(Icons.Default.Email, null) }, keyboardOptions = KeyboardOptions( keyboardType = KeyboardType.Email, imeAction = ImeAction.Next ), keyboardActions = KeyboardActions( onNext = { focusManager.moveFocus(FocusDirection.Down) } ), isError = errorMessage != null, singleLine = true, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth() ) Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp)) // Password field OutlinedTextField( value = password, onValueChange = { password = it; errorMessage = null }, label = { Text("Password") }, leadingIcon = { Icon(Icons.Default.Lock, null) }, trailingIcon = { IconButton(onClick = { passwordVisible = !passwordVisible }) { Icon( if (passwordVisible) Icons.Default.VisibilityOff else Icons.Default.Visibility, "Toggle password" ) } }, visualTransformation = if (passwordVisible) VisualTransformation.None else PasswordVisualTransformation(), keyboardOptions = KeyboardOptions( keyboardType = KeyboardType.Password, imeAction = ImeAction.Done ), keyboardActions = KeyboardActions( onDone = { focusManager.clearFocus() } ), isError = errorMessage != null, singleLine = true, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth() ) // Error message if (errorMessage != null) { Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp)) Text( errorMessage!!, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall ) } Spacer(Modifier.height(24.dp)) // Login button with loading state Button( onClick = { if (email.isBlank() || password.isBlank()) { errorMessage = "Please fill in all fields" } else { isLoading = true // In real app: viewModel.login(email, password) } }, enabled = !isLoading, modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .height(48.dp) ) { if (isLoading) { CircularProgressIndicator( modifier = Modifier.size(24.dp), color = Color.White, strokeWidth = 2.dp ) } else { Text("Log In") } } Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp)) TextButton(onClick = { /* forgot password */ }) { Text("Forgot Password?") } } }

Settings Screen — Scaffold, LazyColumn, Switch/Checkbox

@Composable fun SettingsScreen(onBackClick: () -> Unit) { var notificationsEnabled by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(true) } var darkMode by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) } var downloadOverWifi by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(true) } var autoUpdate by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(true) } var analytics by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) } Scaffold( topBar = { TopAppBar( title = { Text("Settings") }, navigationIcon = { IconButton(onClick = onBackClick) { Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack, "Back") } } ) } ) { padding -> LazyColumn( modifier = Modifier.padding(padding) ) { // Section header item { Text( "General", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleSmall, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary, modifier = Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp) ) } item { SettingsSwitchItem( title = "Notifications", subtitle = "Receive push notifications", checked = notificationsEnabled, onCheckedChange = { notificationsEnabled = it } ) } item { SettingsSwitchItem( title = "Dark Mode", subtitle = "Use dark theme", checked = darkMode, onCheckedChange = { darkMode = it } ) } item { HorizontalDivider(modifier = Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp)) } // Section header item { Text( "Data", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleSmall, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary, modifier = Modifier.padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp) ) } item { SettingsCheckboxItem( title = "Download over Wi-Fi only", checked = downloadOverWifi, onCheckedChange = { downloadOverWifi = it } ) } item { SettingsCheckboxItem( title = "Auto-update apps", checked = autoUpdate, onCheckedChange = { autoUpdate = it } ) } item { SettingsCheckboxItem( title = "Send analytics", checked = analytics, onCheckedChange = { analytics = it } ) } } } } @Composable fun SettingsSwitchItem( title: String, subtitle: String, checked: Boolean, onCheckedChange: (Boolean) -> Unit ) { Row( modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .clickable { onCheckedChange(!checked) } .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 12.dp), horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween, verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically ) { Column(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) { Text(title, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge) Text( subtitle, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant ) } Switch(checked = checked, onCheckedChange = onCheckedChange) } } @Composable fun SettingsCheckboxItem( title: String, checked: Boolean, onCheckedChange: (Boolean) -> Unit ) { Row( modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .clickable { onCheckedChange(!checked) } .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp), verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically ) { Checkbox(checked = checked, onCheckedChange = onCheckedChange) Spacer(Modifier.width(12.dp)) Text(title, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge) } }

Product Card — Card, Image, Text, Button

@Composable fun ProductCard( name: String, price: String, imageUrl: String, rating: Float, onAddToCart: () -> Unit, modifier: Modifier = Modifier ) { Card( modifier = modifier.width(180.dp), shape = RoundedCornerShape(12.dp), elevation = CardDefaults.cardElevation(defaultElevation = 4.dp) ) { Column { // Product image AsyncImage( model = imageUrl, contentDescription = name, modifier = Modifier .fillMaxWidth() .height(140.dp), contentScale = ContentScale.Crop ) Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)) { // Product name — max 2 lines with ellipsis Text( text = name, style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleSmall, maxLines = 2, overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis ) Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp)) // Rating row Row(verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically) { Icon( Icons.Filled.Star, null, tint = Color(0xFFFFB800), modifier = Modifier.size(16.dp) ) Text( " $rating", style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall, color = Color.Gray ) } Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp)) // Price + Add button Row( modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(), horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween, verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically ) { Text( text = price, style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary ) IconButton( onClick = onAddToCart, modifier = Modifier .size(32.dp) .background( MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary, CircleShape ) ) { Icon( Icons.Default.Add, "Add to cart", tint = Color.White, modifier = Modifier.size(18.dp) ) } } } } } }

Full Example — UserProfile Screen

Here is a realistic screen combining Scaffold, Column, Row, Image, Text, Button, and state hoisting:

@Composable fun UserProfileScreen( user: User, onFollowClick: () -> Unit, onBackClick: () -> Unit ) { Scaffold( topBar = { TopAppBar( title = { Text(user.name) }, navigationIcon = { IconButton(onClick = onBackClick) { Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack, "Back") } } ) } ) { padding -> Column( modifier = Modifier .padding(padding) // Scaffold inner padding .padding(16.dp) // content padding .fillMaxSize(), verticalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(12.dp) ) { // Profile header: avatar + info side by side Row( verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically, horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(16.dp) ) { Image( painter = rememberAsyncImagePainter(user.avatarUrl), contentDescription = "Avatar", modifier = Modifier .size(72.dp) .clip(CircleShape) .background(Color.LightGray), contentScale = ContentScale.Crop ) Column { Text( text = user.name, style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall ) Text( text = user.bio, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant ) } } // Stats row Row( modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(), horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceEvenly ) { StatItem("Posts", user.postCount) StatItem("Followers", user.followerCount) StatItem("Following", user.followingCount) } // Follow button — event flows UP Button( onClick = onFollowClick, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth() ) { Text(if (user.isFollowing) "Unfollow" else "Follow") } } } } @Composable private fun StatItem(label: String, count: Int) { Column(horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally) { Text(text = "$count", fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold, fontSize = 18.sp) Text(text = label, fontSize = 12.sp, color = Color.Gray) } }

questions.title: The Disappearing Counter
questions.description: This counter resets to 0 every time you click. Fix it so the count persists across recompositions.

checks.message: Wrap the state in remember{} to survive recomposition

questions.explanation: Composable functions can be called many times (recomposition). Local variables are recreated each time. remember { mutableStateOf(0) } preserves the value AND triggers recomposition when it changes.
hints: Compose recreates local variables on every recomposition
You need two things: preservation (remember) and observability (mutableStateOf)

questions.title: TextField with State
questions.description: Fill in the blanks to create a TextField that displays and updates user input.

blanks.hint: The state variable holding the text

questions.explanation: A TextField in Compose is a controlled component — you must provide the current value AND handle changes via onValueChange.
hints: The TextField needs to both read and write from the same state variable

questions.title: @Composable Annotation
options: Makes the function run on a background thread
Marks a function that can emit UI and participate in composition/recomposition
Automatically caches the function result
Converts it into a suspend function
questions.explanation: @Composable marks a function as part of the Compose runtime. These functions can call other composable functions, emit UI nodes, and participate in recomposition. They run on the main thread.
hints: Think about what makes Compose functions special vs regular Kotlin functions

questions.title: Modifier Chain
questions.description: What visual result does this Modifier chain produce?
options: A white box (200dp) with "Hello" in center
Blue border → Red border → White center with "Hello"
Three stacked colored boxes, largest white
Compilation error — cannot chain multiple backgrounds
questions.explanation: Modifiers are applied in order, wrapping from outside in. .background(Blue) fills the 200dp area blue. .padding(24dp) insets, then .background(Red) fills the remaining area red. Another padding + white background creates the innermost area. Modifier order matters!
hints: Modifiers are applied sequentially — each one wraps the next
padding before background creates a border effect

questions.title: Profile Card
questions.description: Write a composable that displays a user profile card with a name (bold, 20sp), email (gray, 14sp), and a "Follow" button. Use a Column with 8dp spacing and 16dp padding.
questions.explanation: Column stacks children vertically. Arrangement.spacedBy(8.dp) adds consistent spacing. The card follows state hoisting — the click handler (onFollow) is passed in, not defined inside.
hints: Column arranges children vertically
Use Arrangement.spacedBy for consistent spacing between items

questions.title: Infinite Recomposition Loop
questions.description: This composable causes an infinite loop and freezes the app. Fix it.

checks.message: Use a side effect API for time-based operations
Use delay() or a timer mechanism

questions.explanation: Updating state directly in the composable body triggers recomposition, which runs the body again, which updates state again — infinite loop. State mutations must happen in event handlers or side effects. LaunchedEffect(Unit) runs a coroutine once when the composable enters composition, and delay(1000) waits between updates.
hints: Modifying state in the composable body immediately triggers another recomposition
You need a way to run code over time, not on every recomposition

questions.title: remember vs rememberSaveable
questions.description: When should you use rememberSaveable instead of remember?
options: When the state is a complex object like a list
When the state needs to survive configuration changes (like screen rotation)
When you want better performance
When using State in a ViewModel
questions.explanation: remember survives recomposition but NOT configuration changes (rotation, language change). rememberSaveable saves state to the saved instance state bundle, surviving both recomposition AND configuration changes. Use it for UI state the user would be frustrated to lose (text input, scroll position). ViewModel state already survives configuration changes, so rememberSaveable is for state that lives in the composable itself.
hints: What happens to remember{} state when the user rotates the phone?
Think about the saved instance state bundle

questions.title: Basic LazyColumn
questions.description: Fill in the blanks to create a scrollable list of items.

blanks.hint: DSL function to add a list of items

questions.explanation: LazyColumn is the Compose equivalent of RecyclerView — it only composes visible items, making it efficient for long lists. The items() function inside the LazyColumn block adds items from a list. Each item gets a composable lambda.
hints: The "Lazy" prefix means it only renders visible items
The function inside the block specifies what to render for each item

questions.title: Modifier Order Matters
questions.description:
Given this composable, what is the visual difference between modifierA and modifierB? 
val modifierA = Modifier
    .clip(RoundedCornerShape(16.dp))
    .background(Color.Blue)
    .padding(16.dp)

val modifierB = Modifier
    .padding(16.dp)
    .background(Color.Blue)
    .clip(RoundedCornerShape(16.dp))
options: They produce identical results — modifier order does not matter
modifierA: blue rounded box with inner padding. modifierB: 16dp empty space, then blue rounded box with no inner padding
modifierA: rounded corners visible. modifierB: rounded corners invisible because clip is applied after background
modifierB crashes because clip must come before background
questions.explanation: Modifiers wrap from outside in. modifierA: clip rounds the outer shape, background fills with blue inside the clip, padding adds 16dp inside the blue area. modifierB: padding adds 16dp of empty space first, then background fills blue (in the smaller remaining area), then clip rounds the blue. The visible difference: modifierA has padding inside the blue, modifierB has padding outside the blue.
hints: Think of each modifier as wrapping the ones that follow it
padding before background means the padding area has no background color

questions.title: Weight Distribution
questions.description: What does this Row produce on a 360dp wide screen?
questions.code:
Row(modifier = Modifier.width(360.dp)) {
    Box(
        modifier = Modifier
            .weight(1f)
            .height(50.dp)
            .background(Color.Red)
    )
    Box(
        modifier = Modifier
            .width(60.dp)
            .height(50.dp)
            .background(Color.Green)
    )
    Box(
        modifier = Modifier
            .weight(2f)
            .height(50.dp)
            .background(Color.Blue)
    )
}
options: Three equal boxes: 120dp Red, 120dp Green, 120dp Blue
Red=100dp, Green=60dp, Blue=200dp — weight distributes remaining space after fixed elements
Red=120dp, Green=60dp, Blue=180dp — weights divide the full 360dp
Compilation error — cannot mix weight and fixed width in the same Row
questions.explanation: Weight distributes remaining space after fixed-size children are measured. Green takes 60dp fixed. Remaining = 360 - 60 = 300dp. Red (weight 1f) gets 1/3 of 300 = 100dp. Blue (weight 2f) gets 2/3 of 300 = 200dp. Fixed-size children are always measured first.
hints: Fixed-size children (width/size) are measured before weighted children
weight() distributes whatever space is LEFT over

questions.title: Broken Alignment in Box
questions.description: The developer wants a badge (red circle) in the top-right corner of an image, but it appears centered. Fix it.

checks.message: Use Modifier.align(Alignment.TopEnd) on the badge to position it in the top-right corner

questions.explanation: contentAlignment on the parent Box sets the default alignment for ALL children. To override it for a specific child, use Modifier.align() on that child. The badge needs Modifier.align(Alignment.TopEnd) to sit in the top-right corner while the image stays centered.
hints: contentAlignment sets the DEFAULT for all children, but individual children can override it
Look for a modifier that positions a child within a Box

questions.title: Composable Selection
questions.description: You need a list of 10,000 items that scrolls smoothly. Which approach is correct?
options: Column with verticalScroll modifier and 10,000 Text composables
LazyColumn with items(10000) block
ScrollView wrapping a Column (like the View system)
RecyclerView wrapped in AndroidView
questions.explanation: LazyColumn only composes items currently visible on screen (plus a small buffer), making it efficient for large lists. A regular Column with verticalScroll would compose ALL 10,000 items at once, consuming massive memory and causing jank. LazyColumn is the Compose equivalent of RecyclerView and should always be used for large or dynamic lists.
hints: Think about which approach avoids creating 10,000 UI elements at once
The 'Lazy' prefix in Compose means on-demand composition
